function_explanation: Computes a background power spectrum model consisting of two Harvey components plus white noise.
function_docstring:
Compute background power spectrum model with two Harvey components and white noise.

The background model is defined as:
    P(freq) = z1/(1 + (freq/nc1)^2) + z2/(1 + (freq/nc2)^2) + w

where each Harvey component represents granulation noise in stellar power spectra,
and w is the white noise floor.

Args:
    freq: Frequency array at which to evaluate the background model.
        Can be a scalar, list, or numpy array of frequency values.
    z1: Amplitude (zeta) parameter for the first Harvey component.
    nc1: Characteristic frequency (nu_c) for the first Harvey component.
    z2: Amplitude (zeta) parameter for the second Harvey component.
    nc2: Characteristic frequency (nu_c) for the second Harvey component.
    w: White noise level to add to the model.

Returns:
    The background power spectrum evaluated at the input frequencies.
    Has the same shape as the input freq array.
usage_example:
import numpy as np
from agent_bg_model import bg_model

# Define frequency array
freq = np.linspace(0.1, 100, 1000)

# Set Harvey component parameters
z1, nc1 = 1000.0, 10.0  # First granulation component
z2, nc2 = 500.0, 50.0   # Second granulation component
w = 0.5                  # White noise floor

# Compute background model
background = bg_model(freq, z1, nc1, z2, nc2, w)
print(background.shape)  # (1000,)
